meta.name: springboot
meta.title: Springboot Skill
meta.description: Guidelines for building Spring Boot base applications Triggers on: **/*.java, **/*.kt

original_definition.skill_description: Guidelines for building Spring Boot base applications Triggers on: **/*.java, **/*.kt

actions.name: Write code with good maintainability practices, including co
actions.description: Write code with good maintainability practices, including comments on why certain design decisions were made.

actions.name: Handle edge cases and write clear exception handling.
actions.description: Handle edge cases and write clear exception handling.

actions.name: Environment Profiles: Use Spring profiles for different envi
actions.description: Environment Profiles: Use Spring profiles for different environments (dev, test, prod)

actions.name: Package Structure: Organize by feature/domain rather than by
actions.description: Package Structure: Organize by feature/domain rather than by layer

actions.name: If the project uses Maven, run `mvn clean package`.
actions.description: If the project uses Maven, run `mvn clean package`.

actions.name: If the project uses Gradle, run `./gradlew build` (or `gradl
actions.description: If the project uses Gradle, run `./gradlew build` (or `gradlew.bat build` on Windows).

actions.name: | `./gradlew bootJar`       |`./mvnw spring-boot:repackage`
actions.description: | `./gradlew bootJar`       |`./mvnw spring-boot:repackage`     | Package the application as a JAR.             |

actions.name: | `./gradlew bootBuildImage`|`./mvnw spring-boot:build-image
actions.description: | `./gradlew bootBuildImage`|`./mvnw spring-boot:build-image`   | Package the application as a container image. |

rules.description: Use constructor injection for all required dependencies.
Services should be stateless and testable.
Service method signatures should use domain IDs or DTOs, not expose repository entities directly unless necessary.
Do not use concrete implementations (Logback, Log4j2) or `System.out.println()` directly.
Use parameterized queries | Always use Spring Data JPA or `NamedParameterJdbcTemplate` to prevent SQL injection.
Validate request bodies and parameters using JSR-380 (`@NotNull`, `@Size`, etc.) annotations and `BindingResult`
After adding or modifying code, verify the project continues to build successfully.
Ensure all tests pass as part of the build.

directives.description: Make only high confidence suggestions when reviewing code changes.
For libraries or external dependencies, mention their usage and purpose in comments.
Use YAML files (`application.yml`) for externalized configuration.
Configuration Properties: Use @ConfigurationProperties for type-safe configuration binding
Secrets Management: Externalize secrets using environment variables or secret management systems
Separation of Concerns: Keep controllers thin, services focused, and repositories simple
Utility Classes: Make utility classes final with private constructors
Use SLF4J for all logging (`private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyClass.class);`).
Use parameterized logging: `logger.info("User {} logged in", userId);`.
